content:
# Joshua Chapter 21 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 244-246 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_021_page_001.jpg`

---

**1** And the heads of the fathers of the Levites drew near unto Eleazar the priest, and unto Joshua the son of Nun, and unto the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Israel.

**2** And they spoke unto them at Shiloh in the land of Canaan, saying, "YHWH commanded by the hand of Moses to give us cities to dwell in, with their pasturelands for our livestock."

**3** And the sons of Israel gave to the Levites from their inheritance, according to the command of YHWH, these cities and their pasturelands.

**4** And the lot came out for the clans of the Kohathites. And the sons of Aaron the priest, who were of the Levites, received by lot from the tribe of Judah, and from the tribe of the Simeonites, and from the tribe of Benjamin, thirteen cities.

**5** And the remaining sons of Kohath received by lot from the clans of the tribe of Ephraim, and from the tribe of Dan, and from the half-tribe of Manasseh, ten cities.

**6** And the sons of Gershon received by lot from the clans of the tribe of Issachar, and from the tribe of Asher, and from the tribe of Naphtali, and from the half-tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, thirteen cities.

**7** The sons of Merari according to their clans received from the tribe of Reuben, and from the tribe of Gad, and from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve cities.

**8** And the sons of Israel gave these cities and their pasturelands to the Levites by lot, as YHWH had commanded by the hand of Moses.

**9** And they gave from the tribe of the sons of Judah, and from the tribe of the sons of Simeon, these cities which are called by name.

**10** And they were for the sons of Aaron, of the clans of the Kohathites, of the sons of Levi, for theirs was the first lot.

**11** And they gave to them Kiriath-arba — the father of Anak, that is, Hebron — in the hill country of Judah, with its pasturelands surrounding it.

**12** But the fields of the city and its villages they gave to Caleb the son of Jephunneh as his possession.

**13** And to the sons of Aaron the priest they gave Hebron, the city of refuge for the manslayer, and its pasturelands, and Libnah and its pasturelands,

**14** and Jattir and its pasturelands, and Eshtemoa and its pasturelands,

**15** and Holon and its pasturelands, and Debir and its pasturelands,

**16** and Ain and its pasturelands, and Juttah and its pasturelands, and Beth-shemesh and its pasturelands — nine cities from these two tribes.

**17** And from the tribe of Benjamin: Gibeon and its pasturelands, Geba and its pasturelands,

**18** Anathoth and its pasturelands, and Almon and its pasturelands — four cities.

**19** All the cities of the sons of Aaron, the priests, were thirteen cities and their pasturelands.

**20** And the clans of the sons of Kohath, the Levites — the remaining sons of Kohath — the cities of their lot were from the tribe of Ephraim.

**21** And they gave to them Shechem, the city of refuge for the manslayer, and its pasturelands in the hill country of Ephraim, and Gezer and its pasturelands,

**22** and Kibzaim and its pasturelands, and Beth-horon and its pasturelands — four cities.

**23** And from the tribe of Dan: Eltekeh and its pasturelands, Gibbethon and its pasturelands,

**24** Aijalon and its pasturelands, Gath-rimmon and its pasturelands — four cities.

**25** And from the half-tribe of Manasseh: Taanach and its pasturelands, and Gath-rimmon and its pasturelands — two cities.

**26** All the cities for the clans of the remaining sons of Kohath were ten, and their pasturelands.

**27** And to the sons of Gershon, of the clans of the Levites, from the half-tribe of Manasseh: Golan in Bashan, the city of refuge for the manslayer, and its pasturelands, and Beeshterah and its pasturelands — two cities.

**28** And from the tribe of Issachar: Kishion and its pasturelands, Daberath and its pasturelands,

**29** Jarmuth and its pasturelands, En-gannim and its pasturelands — four cities.

**30** And from the tribe of Asher: Mishal and its pasturelands, Abdon and its pasturelands,

**31** Helkath and its pasturelands, and Rehob and its pasturelands — four cities.

**32** And from the tribe of Naphtali: Kedesh in Galilee, the city of refuge for the manslayer, and its pasturelands, and Hammoth-dor and its pasturelands, and Kartan and its pasturelands — three cities.

**33** All the cities of the Gershonites according to their clans were thirteen cities and their pasturelands.

**34** And to the clans of the sons of Merari, the remaining Levites, from the tribe of Zebulun: Jokneam and its pasturelands, Kartah and its pasturelands,

**35** Dimnah and its pasturelands, Nahalal and its pasturelands — four cities.

**36** And from the tribe of Reuben: Bezer and its pasturelands, and Jahaz and its pasturelands,

**37** Kedemoth and its pasturelands, and Mephaath and its pasturelands — four cities.

**38** And from the tribe of Gad: Ramoth in Gilead, the city of refuge for the manslayer, and its pasturelands, and Mahanaim and its pasturelands,

**39** Heshbon and its pasturelands, Jazer and its pasturelands — all the cities were four.

**40** All the cities for the sons of Merari according to their clans, the remaining of the clans of the Levites — their lot was twelve cities.

**41** All the cities of the Levites in the midst of the possession of the sons of Israel were forty-eight cities and their pasturelands.

**42** These cities were each city with its pasturelands surrounding it. Thus it was for all these cities.

**43** And YHWH gave to Israel all the land which He had sworn to give to their fathers. And they possessed it and dwelt in it.

**44** And YHWH gave them rest on every side, according to all that He had sworn to their fathers. And not a man of all their enemies stood before them. All their enemies YHWH gave into their hand.

**45** Not a word fell from all the good word which YHWH had spoken unto the house of Israel. All of it came to pass.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **YHWH** (vv. 2, 3, 8, 43, 44, 45): Rendered from the Tetragrammaton (יהוה) without substitution, following LOGOS convention. Traditional English versions render "the LORD."

2. **Pasturelands** (Heb. מִגְרָשׁ, *migrash*): Occurs over 40 times in this chapter. Refers to the open land surrounding a city designated for grazing livestock. Some versions render "suburbs" (KJV) or "common-land," but "pasturelands" captures the agricultural function more precisely.

3. **City of refuge** (Heb. עִיר הַמִּקְלָט, *ir hammiqlat*): The term appears with Hebron (v. 13), Shechem (v. 21), Golan (v. 27), Kedesh (v. 32), Bezer (v. 36 implied), and Ramoth (v. 38). These were designated in Numbers 35 and Joshua 20 as asylum cities for one who kills inadvertently. The Hebrew literally reads "city of the refuge for the manslayer" (עִיר מִקְלַט הָרֹצֵחַ).

4. **Kiriath-arba** (v. 11): Hebrew קִרְיַת אַרְבַּע, literally "city of four" or "city of Arba." The text identifies it as the city of "the father of the Anak" (אֲבִי הָעֲנָק), referring to the progenitor of the Anakim giants. This is equated with Hebron.

5. **Beeshterah** (v. 27): Hebrew בְּעֶשְׁתְּרָה. This is likely a contracted form of Beth-ashterah ("house/temple of Ashtaroth"). The parallel in 1 Chronicles 6:56 reads "Ashtaroth" (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת).

6. **Gath-rimmon** (v. 25): This name appears both for a city from Dan (v. 24) and from the half-tribe of Manasseh (v. 25). The parallel passage in 1 Chronicles 6:55 reads "Bileam" (בִּלְעָם) for the Manassite city. Many scholars consider the repetition of Gath-rimmon in v. 25 a scribal error in transmission, but the Leningrad Codex preserves this reading and we translate accordingly.

7. **Jarmuth** (v. 29): Hebrew יַרְמוּת. The parallel in 1 Chronicles 6:58 reads "Ramoth" (רָאמוֹת). Not to be confused with the Jarmuth of Judah (Joshua 10:3).

8. **Sons of Israel** (vv. 1, 3, 8, 41): Hebrew בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל, rendered literally. LOGOS convention retains "sons" rather than the interpretive "children" or "people."

9. **Verse structure**: This chapter follows a formulaic allocation list. The Hebrew text uses a highly repetitive pattern of "city + pasturelands" (עִיר + מִגְרָשֶׁיהָ) with the conjunction waw, reflecting the administrative/legal register of the land-distribution narratives.

10. **Verses 43-45**: These three concluding verses form one of the most theologically significant summaries in Joshua, affirming the complete fulfillment of YHWH's land promises to the patriarchs. The phrase "not a word fell" (לֹא־נָפַל דָּבָר) is a striking idiom — the "good word" of YHWH stands firm; none of it drops to the ground unfulfilled.
